nl: In the four-element type Basis, the complement of {e1, e2} is {e3, e4}.
context:
set_option autoImplicit false

inductive Basis : Type
| e1
| e2
| e3
| e4
deriving DecidableEq

def swap12 : Basis → Basis
| Basis.e1 => Basis.e2
| Basis.e2 => Basis.e1
| b => b

def FixedPoints (f : Basis → Basis) : Set Basis := {b | f b = b}
expected: (((Set.singleton Basis.e1 ∪ Set.singleton Basis.e2) : Set Basis)ᶜ) = ((Set.singleton Basis.e3 ∪ Set.singleton Basis.e4) : Set Basis)

provenance.notes: Graduate-level-flavored set equalities over finite carriers; designed for deterministic extensional checking.

nl: For the function swap12 on Basis, the fixed points are exactly {e3, e4}.
context:
set_option autoImplicit false

inductive Basis : Type
| e1
| e2
| e3
| e4
deriving DecidableEq

def swap12 : Basis → Basis
| Basis.e1 => Basis.e2

| b => b

expected: (FixedPoints swap12 : Set Basis) = ((Set.singleton Basis.e3 ∪ Set.singleton Basis.e4) : Set Basis)

nl: For swap12 on Basis, fixed points together with non-fixed points give the universal set.
context:
set_option autoImplicit false

inductive Basis : Type
| e1
| e2
| e3
| e4
deriving DecidableEq

def swap12 : Basis → Basis
| Basis.e1 => Basis.e2
| Basis.e2 => Basis.e1
| b => b

def FixedPoints (f : Basis → Basis) : Set Basis := {b | f b = b}
expected: ((FixedPoints swap12 : Set Basis) ∪ (FixedPoints swap12 : Set Basis)ᶜ : Set Basis) = (Set.univ : Set Basis)
